description:
A 4-building residential campus on a 5,400 m² site in southern Warsaw.
The flagship Green Terrace block (BLD-01) was occupied March 2026;
three sibling blocks (BLD-02, BLD-03, BLD-04) are at progressively
earlier stages of design and construction as the campus rolls out
over 2026-2029.

sharedInfrastructure.description: Central air-source heat-pump plant feeding all 4 buildings via insulated buried mains
sharedInfrastructure.capacity: 320 kW thermal nominal, expandable to 400 kW for BLD-04 connection

sharedInfrastructure.description: Surface lot for 64 cars (22 BLD-01 + 28 BLD-02 + 14 BLD-03/04 shared)
SuDS — permeable paving, swales, and an underground attenuation tank discharging at greenfield rate
sharedInfrastructure.capacity: 1 in 100 year storm with 30% climate-change allowance

sharedInfrastructure.description: Fibre-to-the-apartment via shared campus head-end; one ISP onboard, second TBC

masterPlan.reference: Green Terrace Park — Master Plan, Rev. C (Approved 2024-11-20)
masterPlan.phasingStrategy:
Phase 1: BLD-01 (Green Terrace) — handover March 2026 (complete)
Phase 2a: BLD-02 (Sunny Crescent) — handover September 2027
Phase 2b: BLD-03 (Linden Court) — handover June 2028
Phase 3:  BLD-04 (Oak Pavilion) — handover December 2029 (subject to BLD-03 sales velocity)

sources.title: Green Terrace Park Master Plan Rev. C
sources.author: Green Development Sp. z o.o. + Nowak Architecture

changelog.description: Initial Campus entity — added with the Campus showcase example, closes the last 1-of-27 entity-type gap
